title: SSH username to putty doesn't include domain when set
author: skewty
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->

## Expected Behavior
if username is set to `john` and domain is set to `acme.local` then putty should receive a username of `john@acme.local`

## Current Behavior
if username is set to `john` and domain is set to `acme.local` then putty receives a username of `john` (domain is ignored)

## Possible Solution
include the domain value if it is not blank

## Steps to Reproduce (for bugs)
<!--- Provide an unambiguous set...

iterations.description: PuttyBase.cs SSH path (line 499) reads only Username but ignores Domain when building the -l argument for PuTTY, while the tab-title code (line 294) correctly combines domain+username.

notes:
AI triage (claude): PuttyBase.cs SSH path (line 499) reads only Username but ignores Domain when building the -l argument for PuTTY, while the tab-title code (line 294) correctly combines domain+username.
Approach: After reading username, also read domain and if non-empty prepend 'domain\' to username before passing to -l argument (mirroring the tab-title logic at line 294-306)
